description: Blind A/B routing corpus for the semantic-first experiment. Two segments: (1) international/paraphrase cases that express the SAME intents as routing-benchmark.json but in plain non-native English avoiding the trigger keywords the deterministic pre-router matches on; (2) a sample drawn from routing-benchmark.json spanning all tiers including false-positive guards. Combined the corpus measures whether reading-for-intent-first (Arm B) recovers the long tail of paraphrased requests without regressing the safety-critical force-routes (pr-workflow, security-review) or re-introducing false positives. Each paraphrase case avoids the exact trigger phrases listed in skills/INDEX.json so pre-route.py falls through and the semantic router is the only path that can recover the route. Extension (v1.1, feat/route-ab-harness): 50 additional cases in five new buckets — stub-tier (skills with zero recorded routes; tiered-manifest tripwire), sibling-disambiguation, pipeline-pick (optional expected_pipeline; near-misses must return pipeline null), vague-interview (underspecified requests that should land planning), and plain-english (non-jargon phrasing). Optional fields: expected_pipeline (default null), acceptable (list of {agent, skill} alternates), uncertain (best-effort gold label). Absence of the optional fields means legacy behavior; the 49 legacy cases are byte-identical to v1.0.

test_cases.request: send my commits to the server
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Real git push intent, no 'push'/'GitHub' keyword. MUST route pr-workflow so the commit->quality-gate guarantee holds. pre-route falls through (no trigger).

test_cases.request: save my work to the shared repository
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. 'save to shared repository' = push. No trigger keyword. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: I'd like to submit my changes for review
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. 'submit changes for review' = open a PR. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: please record what I changed into version control
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. 'record into version control' = commit. MUST route pr-workflow (commit intent).

test_cases.request: I want to upload my finished work so the team can see it
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Plain phrasing of push-for-team-visibility = push/PR. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: can you check if the automated tests on my submitted change went green
test_cases.notes: SAFETY-adjacent. CI status on a PR; benchmark routes ci-check intent to pr-workflow. No 'CI'/'PR' token. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: look over my code for safety problems
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Security review intent, no 'security' token. security-review FORCE agent=reviewer-system. Defensible equivalent: parallel-code-review security pass.

test_cases.request: check whether anything here is unsafe before I share it
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. 'is anything unsafe' = security review. No 'security' keyword. MUST route security-review (or defensible parallel-code-review).

test_cases.request: I worry someone could break into this, can you inspect it
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Vulnerability/intrusion concern = security review. No trigger keyword.

test_cases.request: the program stops working when two things happen at the same time, help me find why
test_cases.notes: Race-condition debug, no language stated, no 'debug' token. workflow umbrella owns the debug pipeline. Defensible: socratic-debugging.

test_cases.request: something is wrong with this function and I cannot work out the cause
test_cases.notes: Root-cause investigation, no 'debug'/'bug' trigger phrase that force-routes. workflow debug pipeline. Defensible: socratic-debugging.

test_cases.request: my Go service slowly eats memory until it falls over, find the cause
test_cases.notes: Go memory leak (goroutine leak) phrased without 'goroutine'/'leak' triggers. Domain is explicit (Go). Should reach golang-general-engineer + go-patterns.

test_cases.request: this chunk of logic is repeated everywhere, help me tidy it into one place
test_cases.notes: Extract-duplicate refactor, no 'refactor'/'extract' trigger. workflow owns systematic-refactoring. Defensible: quick if judged contained.

test_cases.request: the structure of this module is messy, reorganize it without changing behaviour
test_cases.notes: Behaviour-preserving restructure = refactor, no trigger keyword. workflow refactor pipeline.

test_cases.request: please write some checks that prove this Python parser behaves correctly
test_cases.notes: 'write checks that prove correctness' = write tests. Python domain explicit. python-general-engineer. Defensible: test-driven-development / python-quality-gate.

test_cases.request: I need automated checks so future edits do not break this feature
test_cases.notes: Regression test intent, no language, no 'test'/'pytest' trigger. test-driven-development or workflow. Defensible: any test-authoring skill.

test_cases.request: have a careful read through my recent edits and tell me what could be better
test_cases.notes: Code review of a diff, no 'review code' trigger phrasing that force-routes. systematic-code-review. Defensible: parallel-code-review.

test_cases.request: go through what I wrote and point out mistakes from a few angles
test_cases.notes: 'from a few angles' = multi-perspective review. parallel-code-review or systematic-code-review both defensible. 'go through' must NOT route golang.

test_cases.request: help me understand how this codebase is put together
test_cases.notes: Read-only architecture exploration, no edit intent. read-only-ops or codebase-analyzer. MUST NOT route an editing skill.

test_cases.request: walk me through what each part of this project does, do not change anything
test_cases.notes: Explicit no-edit exploration. read-only-ops. Defensible: codebase-analyzer / workflow explore pipeline.

test_cases.request: give me a map of where the important files live in here
test_cases.notes: Codebase-map exploration, read-only. read-only-ops or codebase-analyzer.

test_cases.request: moderate the reports waiting in my subreddit queue
test_cases.notes: Reddit modqueue task phrased plainly. reddit-moderate. Distinguishes 'moderate'/'queue' domain from code review.

test_cases.request: before I share my branch, make sure I did not leave any passwords or keys in the code
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Secret-leak scan = security review, no 'security' token. security-review. Defensible: parallel-code-review security pass.

test_cases.request: I think there is a small mistake in the spelling inside main.go, fix it
test_cases.notes: Trivial typo fix phrased plainly (no 'typo' trigger). quick. Tests that plain phrasing of a trivial op still lands light, not on workflow.

test_cases.request: push my changes to GitHub
test_cases.notes: SAFETY control. Keyword form; pre-route fires high-confidence. Anchors that A keeps git safety.

test_cases.request: commit these files
test_cases.notes: SAFETY control. Keyword commit; pre-route high-confidence.

test_cases.request: did CI pass on my PR?
test_cases.notes: SAFETY control. CI status keyword form; pre-route fires pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: clean up my merged branches
test_cases.notes: Keyword branch cleanup; pre-route force-route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: push back on this approach
test_cases.notes: GUARD. Disagreement, not git push. MUST NOT route pr-workflow. Semantic guard exists in pre-route; B must also resist.

test_cases.request: commit to this approach and move forward
test_cases.notes: GUARD. Decision/dedication, not git commit. MUST NOT route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: go ahead and fix the bug
test_cases.notes: GUARD. 'go' is a verb, not Go language. MUST NOT route golang-general-engineer.

test_cases.request: fish out the root cause of this bug
test_cases.notes: GUARD. Search/investigate, not Fish shell. MUST NOT route fish-shell-config.

test_cases.request: give me a quick read on the architecture
test_cases.notes: GUARD. Brief overview, not a 'quick' edit task. MUST NOT force-route quick.

test_cases.request: go ahead and merge the branches in your head
test_cases.notes: GUARD. Cognitive merge, not git merge. MUST NOT route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: we need to scan and remove voice samples from the corpus
test_cases.notes: GUARD. Data cleaning, not voice-writer generation. MUST NOT force-route voice-writer.

test_cases.request: debug the goroutine leak in the worker pool
test_cases.notes: Keyword Go debug; pre-route force-routes go-patterns. Control for Go domain force-route.

test_cases.request: write table-driven tests for the parser
test_cases.notes: Keyword Go tests; force-route go-patterns via 'table-driven'.

test_cases.request: add auth middleware to the Python Flask API
test_cases.notes: Python domain task; candidate tier. python-general-engineer expected (no force-route fires).

test_cases.request: run ruff and mypy on this Python project
test_cases.notes: Python quality checks; candidate tier. python-quality-gate.

test_cases.request: fix the React component rendering bug
test_cases.notes: React/frontend domain; candidate tier. typescript-frontend-engineer.

test_cases.request: comprehensive code review from multiple perspectives
test_cases.notes: Multi-reviewer review; systematic-code-review or parallel-code-review defensible.

test_cases.request: deploy the app to Kubernetes with Helm
test_cases.notes: K8s domain; candidate tier. kubernetes-helm-engineer.

test_cases.request: roast this design doc
test_cases.notes: Niche skill; pre-route does not force-route. LLM must recognize 'roast' as multi-persona critique.

test_cases.request: plan the implementation for this feature
test_cases.notes: Feature plan phase; llm_only. feature-lifecycle or planning defensible. No false-positive force-route expected.

test_cases.request: research the topic thoroughly then write an article
test_cases.notes: Multi-skill chain; research then voice-writer. research-pipeline or voice-writer defensible. LLM resolves chain.

test_cases.request: create a new Claude Code skill with quality gates
test_cases.notes: Skill creation; skill-creator (or skill-creation-pipeline) with toolkit-governance-engineer.

test_cases.request: scaffold a new agent for Rust development
test_cases.notes: Agent creation; force-route agent-creator.

test_cases.request: what's my current branch?
test_cases.notes: Trivial git status; router handles directly, no force-route. MUST NOT route pr-workflow (read-only status, not a git mutation).

test_cases.request: give me your opinion on this design
test_cases.notes: Opinion/recommendation; NOT trivial, must not force-route any skill.

test_cases.request: write Playwright end-to-end tests that walk through the signup form in app/routes/signup.tsx and check the confirmation screen
test_cases.notes: STUB (zero recorded routes). Playwright E2E = e2e-testing per INDEX. Tiered manifest must keep it reachable.

test_cases.request: the checkout pod in the staging namespace keeps restarting with CrashLoopBackOff, figure out what is wrong
test_cases.notes: STUB. Pod failure debugging = kubernetes-debugging per INDEX.

test_cases.request: my Kotlin Flow collector in SyncService.kt stops emitting after the first error, fix the coroutine handling
test_cases.notes: STUB. Flow/coroutine error handling = kotlin-coroutines per INDEX.

test_cases.request: add PHPUnit tests with a database fixture for the InvoiceRepository class in src/Repository/InvoiceRepository.php
test_cases.notes: STUB. PHPUnit + database testing = php-testing per INDEX.

test_cases.request: convert the completion-handler callbacks in NetworkClient.swift to async/await and make the cache actor-safe
test_cases.notes: STUB. async/await + Actor migration = swift-concurrency per INDEX.

test_cases.request: take the renders in assets/frames/ and the narration in voiceover.mp3 and stitch them into an mp4
test_cases.notes: STUB. FFmpeg video from images + audio = image-to-video per INDEX.

test_cases.request: build an HTML slide deck that runs in the browser for my quarterly review talk, one slide per milestone
test_cases.notes: STUB. Browser HTML presentation = frontend-slides per INDEX.

test_cases.request: translate docs/user-guide.md into Japanese and keep the code blocks untouched
test_cases.notes: STUB. Document translation = translate per INDEX.

test_cases.request: post this announcement as a thread on X with the screenshot attached
test_cases.notes: STUB. Tweet/thread/media via X API = x-api per INDEX (agent=python-general-engineer binding).

test_cases.request: go through the cron scripts under ops/cron/ and tell me which ones could fail silently or clobber data
test_cases.notes: STUB. Cron reliability/safety audit = cron-job-auditor per INDEX. Defensible: forensics.

test_cases.request: run the Vitest suite and tell me which tests fail and why
test_cases.notes: STUB. Run Vitest + parse failures = vitest-runner per INDEX.

test_cases.request: add a tilemap level with arcade physics and a jumping player sprite to my Phaser 3 game in src/scenes/
test_cases.notes: STUB. Phaser 3 scenes/physics/tilemaps = phaser-gamedev per INDEX.

test_cases.request: I have three competing proposals in docs/auth-redesign.md — tear them apart from several philosophical angles and tell me where they break
test_cases.notes: Sibling: multi-persona-critique vs professional-communication. Critique of proposals from personas = multi-persona-critique. Defensible: roast.

test_cases.request: turn my rambling notes about last night's outage into a tidy status update I can send to management
test_cases.notes: Sibling: professional-communication vs multi-persona-critique vs productivity. Transform technical notes into business format = professional-communication.

test_cases.request: before we build the export feature, pin down the requirements and acceptance criteria — there is a lot I have not decided yet
test_cases.notes: Sibling: planning vs productivity. Spec/requirements/acceptance criteria = planning umbrella.

test_cases.request: help me sort out my week — I have four deadlines, two meetings to prep, and no idea what to tackle first
test_cases.notes: Sibling: productivity vs planning. Personal task/weekly planning = productivity, not software planning.

test_cases.request: do a careful structured review of the changes in src/billing/ — understand them first, then assess risks and write up findings
test_cases.notes: Sibling: systematic-code-review vs parallel-code-review. Single methodical 4-phase review = systematic-code-review.

test_cases.request: review this diff with separate reviewers for security, business logic, and architecture running at the same time
test_cases.notes: Sibling: parallel-code-review vs systematic-code-review. Explicit concurrent 3-reviewer split = parallel-code-review.

test_cases.request: review my Go error handling in internal/store/ against community conventions for wrapped errors
test_cases.notes: Sibling: go-patterns vs agent-only. Go convention/pattern review = go-patterns skill.

test_cases.request: add a new gRPC endpoint to the Go service in cmd/api that returns user quotas
test_cases.notes: Sibling: agent-only vs go-patterns. Plain Go feature work = golang-general-engineer, no pattern skill needed.

test_cases.request: here are ten of my old blog posts, build a voice profile from them so future articles sound like me
test_cases.notes: Sibling: create-voice vs voice-validator vs voice-writer. Profile FROM samples = create-voice.

test_cases.request: check whether this draft actually sounds like my voice profile, and fix the spots that do not
test_cases.notes: Sibling: voice-validator vs create-voice vs voice-writer. Fidelity check + rewrite loop = voice-validator.

test_cases.request: run a formal research effort on local-first sync engines — scope it, gather sources with quality gates, and save the artifacts
test_cases.notes: Sibling: research-pipeline vs read-only exploration. Formal phased research with artifacts = research-pipeline.

test_cases.request: map out the architecture of this repo — modules, boundaries, and how data flows between them, no edits
test_cases.notes: Sibling: codebase-overview vs read-only-ops vs research-pipeline. Architecture mapping of LOCAL code = codebase-overview; read-only-ops defensible; research-pipeline wrong (external research).

test_cases.request: write a full blog post about the database migration in my own voice, and run the voice validation and joy check before you call it done
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE true-match. Voice content with mandatory validation = voice-writer pipeline.

test_cases.request: research WebGPU adoption end to end — scope, gather, synthesize, validate — and deliver a cited report with saved artifacts
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE true-match. Names the 5 phases; research-pipeline.

test_cases.request: run the full multi-wave review across every package in this repo and give me a prioritized issue backlog
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE true-match. Whole-repo multi-wave review = comprehensive-review pipeline; full-repo-review skill is the closest skill label.

test_cases.request: research how indie game studios fund their first title, then turn the findings into an article in my voice
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE true-match. Research then voice article = research-to-article pipeline (chains research + voice-writer).

test_cases.request: fix the two typos in my published blog post about Kubernetes
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Blog-adjacent but trivial edit; pipeline MUST be null, not voice-writer/publish.

test_cases.request: what is the difference between server-sent events and websockets? just a quick answer, no report
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE near-miss. One-line factual answer; pipeline MUST be null, not research-pipeline.

test_cases.request: review just this one function in utils/parse.py before I merge
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Single-function review; pipeline MUST be null, not comprehensive-review.

test_cases.request: summarize yesterday's standup notes into three bullet points
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Trivial summary; pipeline MUST be null, not research-to-article or voice-writer.

test_cases.request: give me a quick sense of what lives in the scripts/ folder
test_cases.notes: PIPELINE near-miss. Light read-only peek; pipeline MUST be null, not explore-pipeline or comprehensive-review.

test_cases.request: make the app better
test_cases.notes: VAGUE. No target, no criteria. Should land planning/interview mode to resolve ambiguity, not start editing.

test_cases.request: I want to add some AI stuff to my site, you pick what makes sense
test_cases.notes: VAGUE. Open-ended feature wish; needs scoping questions first = planning.

test_cases.request: this project needs a refresh, where do we even start
test_cases.notes: VAGUE. 'Refresh' undefined; planning interview before any work.

test_cases.request: can you improve things so users stop complaining
test_cases.notes: VAGUE. No symptom named; must interview, not guess a fix.

test_cases.request: I have an idea for a feature but I am not sure what it should do exactly
test_cases.notes: VAGUE but clear destination: 'what should this do' is a planning trigger (spec/requirements).

test_cases.request: do something about the performance, I guess?
test_cases.notes: VAGUE. No metric, no surface named. Interview first; jumping to an optimization agent is premature.

test_cases.request: we should probably clean things up around here
test_cases.notes: VAGUE. 'Clean up' could be code, files, or process; interview first.

test_cases.request: make the site look less boring
test_cases.notes: PLAIN. Visual distinctiveness ask without design jargon = ui-design-engineer + distinctive-frontend-design.

test_cases.request: the page takes forever to show up on my phone
test_cases.notes: PLAIN. Slow page load without 'performance'/'Core Web Vitals' jargon = performance-optimization-engineer.

test_cases.request: before I put this on the internet, check that nobody can log in as somebody else
test_cases.notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. Auth-bypass concern without 'security' token = security-review.

test_cases.request: put my website on the internet so other people can see it
test_cases.notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. Public exposure without 'deploy'/'DNS' jargon = public-web-deploy (FORCE skill).

test_cases.request: turn these words into Spanish but leave the computer code alone
test_cases.notes: PLAIN. Translation without 'translate the document' phrasing; code blocks preserved = translate.

test_cases.request: my program worked yesterday and today it does not, nothing changed I swear
test_cases.notes: PLAIN. Regression debugging without 'debug'/'bug' tokens = workflow debug pipeline. Defensible: socratic-debugging.

test_cases.request: save what I did so it does not get lost if my laptop dies
test_cases.notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. Commit/push intent with zero git vocabulary. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: write down how this thing works so the next person is not lost
test_cases.notes: PLAIN. Documentation ask without 'docs'/'README' tokens = technical-documentation-engineer.

test_cases.request: the computer yells at me with red text when I run the tests, make it stop
test_cases.notes: PLAIN. Failing tests phrased as 'red text' = debug the failures (workflow). Defensible: test-driven-development.

test_cases.request: make my pull request nice so my boss approves it
test_cases.notes: SAFETY, PLAIN. PR preparation in casual words. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: stage the modified files and commit them
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Real git stage+commit intent. MUST route pr-workflow so quality gates fire.

test_cases.request: add my changes to staging and make a commit
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Paraphrase of stage+commit. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: take what I changed and save it as a commit
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Non-technical stage+commit paraphrase. MUST route pr-workflow.

test_cases.request: audit the auth flow for vulnerabilities
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Real security audit intent. MUST route security-review, not finance. Bare 'audit' unigram previously misrouted to finance.

test_cases.request: check the authentication code for security holes
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Auth security review paraphrase. MUST route security-review.

test_cases.request: look for vulnerabilities in my login system
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Login security audit paraphrase. MUST route security-review.

test_cases.request: run a financial audit of last quarter's journal entries
test_cases.notes: Financial audit should route to finance, not security-review. Disambiguation: 'financial' qualifier.

test_cases.request: commit the staged changes and push to remote
test_cases.notes: SAFETY. Commit+push intent. MUST route pr-workflow.
